findings.severity_rationale: str.format of form username/password into SELECT (libuser.py:12); pre-auth auth-bypass (username="' OR '1'='1")
%-format of form username/password into INSERT (libuser.py:25); pre-auth SQLi

form 'password' str.format into UPDATE (libuser.py:53); the session is UNSIGNED base64 (libsession.py:6-7) => forgeable => de-facto pre-auth; injection changes arbitrary users' passwords => account takeover
findings.notes: guarded is structural; forgeable session drives severity to critical

findings.guard_evidence: bad/mod_user.py:9 — session created at public /login via libsession.create
findings.severity_rationale: untrusted username placed in an UNSIGNED base64 session cookie with no MAC (libsession.py:6-7); attacker forges {'username':'admin'} => session forgery/auth bypass
findings.notes: MULTI-CLASS: CodeQL rule=cookie-injection (secsev 5.0) but base64 neutralizes header-injection; the REAL issue is broken session integrity (forgery). Labelled real by the forgery impact

findings.guard_evidence: good/vulpy-ssl.py:29 — app.run(debug=True) is the app entrypoint
findings.severity_rationale: Flask debug=True but bound to host='127.0.1.1' (localhost) -> Werkzeug debugger not remotely reachable; medium (contrast pygoat[14] on 0.0.0.0 = high)
findings.notes: present in BOTH bad/ and good/ trees (good/ did not remove debug=True)

findings.guard_evidence: bad/api_post.py — CLI API client, not a web route
findings.severity_rationale: client writes its received API key to a file in cleartext (api_post.py:27); hygiene, local file, client's own secret
client prints its API key to stdout (api_post.py:24); cleartext logging of a secret, low impact

findings.guard_evidence: not a web route (standalone CLI); guard n/a
findings.severity_rationale: FP: the flagged value is operator-local — the printed password is a HARDCODED list element (brute.py:9-18), not a captured credential; not a captured or app-issued secret (contrast api_post.py [8][9] which mishandle a server-issued api_key). Verdict FP by source analysis, not by 'it is test code'.

findings.guard_evidence: bad/mod_api.py:30 — POST /key; libapi.keygen requires a valid login (libapi.py:10-12)
findings.severity_rationale: user 'username' str.format into a /tmp path then Path.touch + Path.glob(...).unlink (libapi.py:16-22); '../' traverses => create/DELETE arbitrary files; login gate is SQLi-bypassable ([0])
